branch_metadata.branch_rationale: Einstein-equation source residual / GR emergence diagnostic; T_00, G_00, Lambda_t are matter-side / source-side per P4 manuscript

description: Einstein-identity gap Delta_E(N) convergence: two-point Richardson extrapolation across the only two clean lattice sizes currently available (N1=1534 in the canonical regime; N2=2254 in the extended regime). Three independent candidate exponents — analytical Ricci-order (alpha=2/3), conservative (alpha=1.0), and empirical 2-point fit (alpha=0.8477) — all yield a continuum-limit gap below the 0.05 closure-domain threshold. A definitive single-exponent identification requires >=3 independent N points; that data is in flight in a parallel lattice run.

two_point_diagnostics.comment: gap decreases as N increases, consistent with continuum-limit convergence

richardson_candidates.rationale: From the Ricci-tensor-leading discretization argument; analytical structural target
Direct power-law fit on the 2 available points; not yet independently determined because >=3 points are required to fix alpha

data_poverty_caveat: Only 2 N-points are clean at the present audit date; >=3 N-points are required to independently fix alpha and run a Richardson stability analysis. Lattice runs at N approximately 3500-9400 (regimes P3-P8) are in flight and will replace this two-point Richardson with a multi-point fit when complete.
